events: Michigan Consumer Sentiment Final — Tue Jun 23, 10:00am ET
Micron Technology (MU) earnings — Wed Jun 24, after close
PCE / Personal Income & Outlays (May) — Thu Jun 25, 8:30am ET
Initial Jobless Claims — Thu Jun 25, 8:30am ET
fed_note: FOMC held at 3.50-3.75% on Jun 17, 2026. Median dot projects 3.8% by year-end — a hike is possible. New chair Kevin Warsh's first meeting concluded with no change but a hawkish lean.

inflation.note: CPI rose 4.2% YoY in May — highest since April 2023 — driven by a 23.5% surge in energy. May PCE releases Thursday Jun 25.

valuation.note: Shiller CAPE at 39.9 sits in the 98th percentile of all historical readings — market is historically expensive.

sentiment.label: Fear
sentiment.note: CNN Fear & Greed at 37 (Fear zone) — investors are nervous despite SPY near highs.

geopolitics.us_iran: Ceasefire / peace deal framework agreed mid-June. Hormuz reopening expected, driving crude oil down sharply (WTI ~$81, Brent ~$84). Reduces energy inflation tail risk but negotiations still in progress.
geopolitics.oil_impact: Oil prices at 3-month lows after deal. Positive for inflation outlook if sustained.

futures_am.tone: Flat to mildly lower premarket. AI trade cooling slightly. Market digesting last week's post-Fed selloff.

note: Market is expensive by most measures (Shiller CAPE at 98th percentile, trailing PE 25.5x), inflation is running hot at 4.2% CPI, the Fed has a mild hike bias, and a big inflation print (May PCE) lands Thursday. Futures are flat/slightly red. The one positive: US-Iran deal has knocked crude oil to 3-month lows, reducing the energy inflation tail. Fear/Greed at 37 shows investor nervousness despite SPY above its 200-day average. On balance, the macro environment warrants caution — demand higher discounts before any buy.
